summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host info and wallet data to attacker-controlled C2
details: Package name advertises wallet security but the shipped JS files (scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js, build-verifier.js) read host identity (os.hostname(), os/fs enumeration), shell out via child_process/execSync (curl, ping, whoami, docker push), walk filesystem paths with fs.existsSync, and POST/GET to a hardcoded C2 endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (scanner.js:161, scanner.js:323). The combination of wallet-scanning code paths, system-information collection (os.hostname, fs enumeration of likely wallet/config directories), child_process command execution, and exfiltration via https.request/https.get to an attacker-controlled github.io site is a credential/wallet stealer. The package name is a lure for users searching for wallet security tooling; installing or running it ships installer host data and wallet-related filesystem contents to the operator of ddjidd564.github.io.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
